evals.expected_output: A structured technical plan for the /changelog skill with architecture decisions, modules table, phases (Phase 1 as a tracer bullet), and optionally a project rules check. Posted as a GitHub comment on issue #1.

assertions.description: Plan contains a Phases section with at least 2 phases
Phase 1 is explicitly described as a tracer bullet or thin vertical slice
Plan contains a Modules table with at least one row
Plan contains no unresolved markers like [?], [UNKNOWN], or [TBD]
Plan references specific files, packages, or patterns found in the codebase (not just generic placeholders)

evals.prompt: /plan #2 — I want to start implementing the PR review skill
evals.expected_output: A structured technical plan for the /review skill including interface contracts (the review comment structure from the spec), modules, and phases. Posted as a GitHub comment on issue #2.

assertions.description: Plan contains a Phases section with at least 2 phases
Phase 1 is explicitly described as a tracer bullet or thin vertical slice
Plan contains an Interface Contracts section (since the spec explicitly defines a review comment structure)
Plan contains a Modules table with at least one row
Plan contains no unresolved markers like [?], [UNKNOWN], or [TBD]

evals.prompt: I just finished writing the spec in issue #3, can you plan the implementation for me?
evals.expected_output: A structured technical plan for the /sprint-summary skill with dual-platform publishing (GitHub and Confluence), phases, and modules. Posted as a GitHub comment on issue #3.

assertions.description: Plan contains a Phases section with at least 2 phases
Phase 1 is explicitly described as a tracer bullet or thin vertical slice
Plan contains a Modules table with at least one row
Plan contains no unresolved markers like [?], [UNKNOWN], or [TBD]
Plan addresses the dual publishing destination (GitHub and Confluence) mentioned in the spec
